narrative:
The Zurich Zebras are positioned for a high-ceiling offensive output today, anchored by Kyle Schwarber, who carries our day-high 104.27 projection against Tomoyuki Sugano. Schwarber benefits from a massive 14% park factor boost and an elite platoon advantage, making him the centerpiece of our 5x5 Roto effort. He is supported by strong outings from Trea Turner and Caleb Durbin, both of whom draw favorable matchups against right-handed starters, leveraging significant SP Skill differentials to drive run production and maintain our stolen base pacing.

We remain cautious regarding our left-on-left matchups, as Yordan Alvarez and Christian Yelich face regression due to substantial platoon penalties. Yelich, in particular, enters the lineup as a borderline play given his historical BvP struggles against Kris Bubic; his reliance on situational hitting necessitates a close monitor of game-time wind patterns. While several starters are boosted by "Wind Out" conditions, we are prioritizing high-OPS upside over total roster depth to ensure we maximize our efficiency in home run and RBI categories for this early-April slate.
